title: 10 best herbal creams for frizzy hair

remedy_properties: Emollient Properties: Herbal aloe creams contain emollients that fill in gaps between hair cuticles, smoothing the hair surface and reducing frizz by allowing hair to lie flat against each other.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe irritated scalps and reduce inflammation caused by styling tools or environmental stressors, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Hydrating Properties: Herbal aloe creams are rich in moisturizing agents that deeply hydrate the hair shaft, reducing dryness and brittleness that can cause frizz and flyaways.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that provide hydration and nourishment. Aloe vera contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium, copper, and zinc. These nutrients work to lock in moisture, smooth the hair cuticle, and add shine. The gel-like texture of aloe vera cream helps to tame frizzy hair by filling in gaps and allowing the hair to lie flat, resulting in a smoother, more manageable finish. Regular use can leave hair looking healthy and silky.
remedy_constituents: Amino Acids: Amino acids, specifically glycine and alanine, help with frizzy hair by repairing and strengthening the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and improving hair elasticity.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpene glycoside, help with frizzy hair by creating a protective barrier on the hair surface, reducing moisture loss and frizz, and leaving the hair feeling smooth and silky.

Vitamins (specifically Vitamin E): Vitamin E helps with frizzy hair by its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and protect the hair from environmental stressors, reducing frizz and breakage, and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part for hair care due to its high gel content, which provides moisturizing and soothing benefits to frizzy hair.
Gel from Leaves: This part is used in many hair care products as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm frizzy hair and reduce damage.
Extract from Leaves: The extract from aloe vera leaves is also used to make creams for frizzy hair as it contains antioxidants that help repair and protect the hair from damage and breakage.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100 grams of aloe vera gel from the leaves of the plant using a sharp knife.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the mixture of aloe vera gel and oils in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Add 10 grams of shea butter and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cuticles, leading to frizz and dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and dandruff that can contribute to frizzy hair.

Astringent and antifungal properties: The astringent and antifungal properties of lemongrass cream help to balance the scalp's natural pH and combat fungal infections that can cause flakiness, itchiness, and frizz.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its nourishing and moisturizing properties. The cream contains citral, a natural compound that helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine. It also contains antioxidants that protect the hair from damage caused by heat styling tools and environmental stressors, leaving the hair feeling soft, smooth, and manageable. This results in a sleeker, more polished look, making it an effective solution for frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A terpene that helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
Geranial: A terpene that has antiseptic and antifungal properties, helping to prevent scalp irritation and reduce frizz caused by dandruff or other scalp conditions.
Cymbopogonolide: A phenolic compound that has antioxidant properties, helping to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, which can lead to frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in citral, a compound that helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz.
Stems: The stems are also a source of essential oils, which help to add shine and manageability to frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of cymbopogon citratus essential oil 5% of the total mixture weight and 500 ml of distilled water.
Combine cymbopogon citratus essential oil with 450 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 40-50 degrees celsius.
Gradually add 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Add 500 ml of distilled water to the mixture and blend using a handheld mixer at low speed for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sanitized container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe irritated scalps, reducing flakiness and inflammation that can contribute to frizz.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cuticles, leading to dryness and frizz, while also protecting the hair follicles from environmental stressors.

Vaso-dilating properties: Stinging nettle's vaso-dilating compounds increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy circulation and reducing the likelihood of dry, brittle strands that lead to frizzy hair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's ability to lock in moisture and reduce frizz is due to its high concentration of beta-sitosterol, a plant sterol that helps to repair and protect the hair's cuticle. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy hair growth environment, resulting in smoother, more manageable hair. This leads to a reduction in frizz and a more polished, healthy-looking hair.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica creams help to improve the hair's texture and reduce frizz by creating a protective barrier on the hair surface, locking in moisture and reducing cuticle lift.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Urtica dioica creams help to reduce frizz by inhibiting the activity of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss and frizz.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Urtica dioica creams help to reduce frizz by scavenging free radicals, which can damage hair follicles and contribute to frizzy hair, and by improving blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and plant compounds like histamine, which help to lock moisture in the hair and reduce frizz.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain high amounts of glycosides and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting smooth hair.
Roots: The roots of the plant are rich in natural oils and compounds that help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the early morning to preserve their potency.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica in a warm place at 30°c for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steep 50g of dried urtica dioica in 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Rosemary's antioxidants help protect the hair cuticle from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and flyaways by promoting healthy hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and dandruff that can contribute to frizzy hair, while also improving blood flow to promote healthy hair growth.

Stimulants: Rosemary's stimulant properties help increase circulation to the scalp, stimulating hair follicles and promoting healthy hair growth, which in turn reduces frizz and flyaways by giving hair a sleeker, smoother appearance.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly camphor and bornyl acetate, help to smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine. Additionally, rosemary's stimulating properties can increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and strengthening follicles. This results in smoother, more manageable hair that is less prone to frizz and more resilient to humidity and environmental stressors.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Helps to reduce frizz by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down the hair's natural lipids, thereby locking in moisture and reducing dryness.
Bornyl acetate: Provides a protective barrier on the hair's surface, smoothing the cuticle and reducing the appearance of frizz, while also helping to lock in moisture and add shine.

Rosmarinic acid: Offers antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage, leading to a reduction in frizz and a healthier, more manageable appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosmarinus officinalis leaves are commonly used in hair creams due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils that help to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are also rich in antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control scalp irritation and promote healthy hair growth.
Leaves: Rosmarinus officinalis leaves contain compounds that help to enhance hair elasticity and reduce breakage, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for best results.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 weeks or until the oil turns fragrant and dark green in color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar to separate the infused oil from the solids.
Whisk together 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness and frizz.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Soothing and calming effects: Lemon balm's natural sedative and calming properties can help calm the hair follicles and reduce stress-induced frizz, leaving hair looking smoother and more manageable.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique combination of properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to frizz. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect the hair from damage caused by heat styling and environmental stressors, leaving it smoother and more manageable. This makes it an effective ingredient in creams designed to tame frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This monoterpene helps reduce frizzy hair by providing a protective barrier against environmental stressors and locking in moisture.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, resulting in smoother and less frizzy hair.
Geraniol: This monoterpene has a calming effect on the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to frizzy hair, and also helps to balance the scalp's natural pH.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Roots: Contain active compounds that help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce frizz.
Stems: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm irritated scalps and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried melissa officinalis flowers at peak potency and store them in airtight containers.
Combine the dried melissa officinalis flowers with 50 grams of distilled water in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture at 70-80 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes to facilitate the infusion process.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix the resulting infusion with 200 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax to create a stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties in pot marigold cream can soothe and calm irritated scalps and hair follicles, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: Pot marigold's emollient and moisturizing properties help to hydrate and smooth the hair cuticle, leaving it soft, shiny, and less prone to frizz.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its exceptional moisturizing and soothing properties. The antioxidants present in Calendula help lock in moisture, reduce dryness, and smooth the hair cuticle, leaving it looking sleek and manageable. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Calendula help calm irritated scalps and reduce tangles, making it easier to comb and style frizzy hair. Regular use of Calendula creams can lead to softer, shinier, and more manageable locks.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help to lock moisture into the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine, while also providing a protective barrier against environmental stressors.
Carotenoids: As antioxidants, carotenoids help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to hair, leading to frizz and breakage, and promote overall hair health.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Leaves: They are rich in vitamins A, B, and E, which help protect hair from damage and promote healthy growth, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Seeds: They contain anti-frizz and smoothing properties that help reduce frizz and add a smooth, silky texture to hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in large quantities for later use.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 1 cup of beeswax and 1 cup of shea butter.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler and let it cool before filling into containers.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help protect the hair from environmental stressors, damage, and dryness, which can contribute to frizziness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can cause irritation, itchiness, and frizziness, while soothing and calming the hair.
Soothing and calming properties: Chamomile's natural sedative and anxiolytic effects help calm the hair follicles, reducing stress-induced frizziness and promoting smooth, healthy-looking hair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its soothing and calming properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile help to smooth and tame frizzy hair, reducing its tendency to become unruly and unmanageable. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, leaving hair soft and silky to the touch. The result is a smoother, more manageable hair texture that is easier to style and maintain. This makes chamomile creams an excellent natural solution for tackling frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and soothing the scalp, thereby minimizing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and adding smoothness to the hair.
Matricarin: This sesquiterpene lactone has antioxidant properties that help protect the hair from environmental damage, reduce frizz, and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used in creams for frizzy hair due to their soothing and calming properties that help reduce inflammation and promote smooth hair.
Seeds: Used in creams for frizzy hair due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce frizz and add shine.
Leaves: Used in creams for frizzy hair due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance scalp pH and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of dried flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 cup of distilled water and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and set.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Hypericin: This phytochemical has antioxidant properties, protecting the hair shaft from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, resulting in smoother, less frizzy locks.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing breakage, which can contribute to frizziness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its rich antioxidant and ant-inflammatory properties. The herb contains hyperforin and hypericin, which help to protect the hair cuticles from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors. This leads to smoother, shinier hair with reduced frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce scalp irritation and inflammation, promoting a healthy hair growth environment.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound helps with frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the scalp, which can lead to smoother, more manageable hair.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has antioxidant properties that help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, resulting in healthier, less frizzy hair.
N-Acetyl-Aspartic Acid (NAA): This amino acid has been shown to help reduce frizz by improving the hair's natural moisture barrier and promoting a smoother hair cuticle.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, which help in reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have antioxidant properties that help in nourishing and protecting hair.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in soothing and smoothing frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a clean environment.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of carrier oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl or double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 2-3 hours or until the oil has turned a dark greenish color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Allow the cream to infuse for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place before using it on the skin.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Dandelion cream contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage hair cuticles and lead to frizz. Antioxidants also help protect the hair shaft from environmental stressors.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream soothe irritated scalps and calm inflammation, reducing frizz caused by irritated hair follicles.

Moisturizing and nourishing fatty acids: Dandelion cream is rich in moisturizing and nourishing fatty acids, such as oleic acid, linoleic acid, and palmitic acid, which help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and leave hair feeling soft and manageable.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with frizzy hair because it contains inulin, a natural humectant that locks in moisture and smooths the hair cuticle. The cream's ability to retain moisture helps to combat dryness and damage, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Taraxacum officinale may help to soothe an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy hair environment that's less prone to frizz and breakage. Regular use can leave hair looking smoother, shinier, and more manageable.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps to stabilize and smooth hair cuticles, reducing frizz and improving hair manageability.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect hair from environmental stressors and damage, leading to less frizz.
Saponins: A type of phytochemical that has been found to have moisturizing and humectant properties, helping to lock in moisture and reduce frizz in dry or damaged hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of inulin, a polysaccharide that helps to smooth and calm the hair.
Seeds: They are used due to their rich content of saponins, which have been reported to help smooth and condition the hair.
Roots: They are used due to their content of inulin and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the hair, reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves at dawn when the dew is still present.
Extract the herbal ingredients in a double boiler using 200 milliliters of water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then add 20 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius and blend until the beeswax is fully incorporated for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify then store it in the refrigerator for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Aloe vera: Soothes the scalp and hair follicles, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to frizz.
Green tea extract: Rich in antioxidants, it helps protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, leading to smoother, less-frizzy locks.
Coconut oil: Moisturizes and nourishes the hair, locking in moisture and creating a barrier against humidity that can cause frizz.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium robertianum, also known as heron's pie, creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to balance the scalp's natural moisture levels. The herb's soothing properties calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to frizz. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Geranium robertianum help repair and protect the hair shaft from environmental stressors, leaving hair smooth, silky, and manageable. Regular use can lead to a reduction in frizz and a more vibrant, healthy-looking mane.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinol: Helps with frizzy hair by smoothing the cuticle and reducing frizz due to its ability to bind to and stabilize the hair's keratin structure.

Rosmarinic acid: Contributes to reducing frizzy hair by its antioxidant properties, which help protect the hair from environmental damage and prevent moisture loss, thus reducing frizz and enhancing smoothness.
Geraniol: Helps with frizzy hair by its emollient and humectant properties, which help lock in moisture and keep the hair hydrated, leading to a smoother and less frizzy appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used in herbal remedies due to their antiseptic and astringent properties.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain compounds that may help to soothe and calm the scalp.
Seeds: The seeds may be used to create a natural hair oil or cream due to their potential emollient and nourishing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh leaves and flowers of the specified herb in early morning for best results.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 1/2 cup of dried plant material in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then mix 1/2 cup of the oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the cream is smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to smooth and tame unruly locks. The anti-inflammatory properties of the herb help to calm the scalp, reducing irritation and soothing dryness that can contribute to frizz. Meanwhile, the moisturizing properties of the cream deeply nourish the hair shaft, leaving it soft, silky, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry creams contain antioxidants like anthocyanins and quercetin, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair cuticles and cause frizz, leading to smoother, more manageable locks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in highbush cranberries, such as salicylic acid and flavonoids, can reduce inflammation in the scalp and hair follicles, which can contribute to frizzy hair by disrupting the natural balance of the scalp's moisture and oil production.

Humectant and emollient properties: The high water content and glycosides in highbush cranberries make them a natural humectant and emollient, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, damaged hair cuticles, reducing frizz and flyaways for softer, more luscious locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, the roots help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to damaged hair.
Barks: Containing compounds like tannins and phenolic acids, the barks work to strengthen hair follicles, reducing breakage and flyaways associated with frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep 1 cup of the berries in 4 cups of boiling distilled water for 20 minutes to create a strong decoction.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 8 ounces of coconut oil and 2 ounces of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the vaccinium macrocarpon decoction to the melted mixture stirring until fully incorporated and cooling for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that work to calm and soothe the scalp. The natural antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents in damask rose help to reduce frizz by controlling excess oil production, smoothing the cuticle layer, and strengthening the hair follicles. Additionally, the moisturizing qualities of damask rose creams deeply nourish the hair, leaving it soft, silky, and manageable. As a result, frizzy hair becomes more manageable and less prone to unruly behavior.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Damask rose creams contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help reduce inflammation in the scalp and prevent damage to the hair follicles, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: The fatty acids and glycerides present in damask rose cream help lock in moisture, nourish, and soften the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways by creating a protective barrier against humidity and heat damage.

Antioxidant and astringent properties: Damask rose extracts contain anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have antioxidant and astringent properties that help stabilize and tighten the hair's cuticle layer, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and essential fatty acids, rose flowers help to nourish and moisturize dry, damaged hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, rose seeds provide a natural source of moisture and hydration to dry, brittle hair, smoothing the cuticle and reducing frizz.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried rosa damascena flowers in sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3 for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 24 hours of resting time.
Combine the strained oil with beeswax, vitamin e oil, and coconut oil in a double boiler at 150°f for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency.
Package the final cream in sterilized containers and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to moisturize and soothe dry, unruly locks. The herbal extracts in mallow cream work to lock in moisture, reducing frizz and flyaways while adding shine and softness to the hair. Additionally, the creamy texture provides a barrier against humidity, helping to smooth out rough ends and tangles. As a result, mallow creams leave hair looking healthy, manageable, and beautifully defined.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Mucilage is a thick, protective substance found in mallows that helps to lock in moisture and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways.

Antioxidants: Mallows contain antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and protect the hair from damage caused by heat styling tools, UV radiation, and other environmental stressors that can contribute to frizz.
Emollients: The emollient properties of mallow creams help to nourish and soften the hair, reducing dryness and brittleness that can lead to frizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, damaged hair.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, the seeds nourish and protect the hair from environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh malva sylvestris leaves and dry them in the shade for three days.
Grind dried malva sylvestris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of ground malva sylvestris with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 milliliters of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. The extracts from wild potatoes contain a natural oil-absorbing property that effectively locks in moisture while also reducing frizz. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory agents help to soothe and calm the scalp, preventing irritation and flyaways. This results in silky, smooth hair that is manageable and easy to style. With regular use, you can say goodbye to frizzy days and hello to luscious locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wild potato creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz and flyaways caused by inflammation.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in wild potato creams protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, helping to smooth and tame frizzy locks.
Moisturizing fatty acids: Wild potato creams contain moisturizing fatty acids that deeply nourish and hydrate the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine to dry, brittle strands.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain antioxidants and natural moisturizers that nourish and hydrate the hair, smoothing out frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh taraxacum kok saghyz leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend the leaves into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the leaf paste with 100ml of coconut oil, 50ml of olive oil, and 20g of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts completely.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with frizzy hair because its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and dryness that can lead to frizz. The cream's antifungal and antibacterial properties also work to control the growth of bacteria and fungus on the scalp, which can contribute to frizzy locks. Additionally, wormwood's natural astringent properties help to balance the scalp's pH, leading to smoother, healthier hair with reduced frizz and flyaways.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidants help protect the hair shaft from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and flyaways by keeping the hair cuticle strong and smooth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness, which can contribute to frizzy hair by disrupting the natural moisture balance of the scalp and hair.

Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent qualities help to tighten the hair follicles and pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and excess sebum that can weigh down the hair and lead to frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, wormwood roots help to soothe and calm frizzy hair by reducing irritation and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of tannins, wormwood barks provide a natural astringent properties that help to reduce frizz by tightening the cuticle layer of the hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried artemisia absinthium leaves from reputable sources for optimal potency.
Combine 1/4 cup of sweet almond oil and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat.
Infuse the oil mixture with 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium leaves for 2 hours, monitoring temperature below 160°f.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container within 10 minutes of straining.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature over 24-48 hours before using on frizzy hair for optimal benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with frizzy hair because they contain natural ingredients that help to smooth and calm the hair cuticle. The herbs found in these creams, such as elderberry and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe dryness and irritation on the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. Additionally, elder creams often contain emollients like coconut oil and shea butter that deeply nourish the hair strands, leaving them soft, silky, and manageable. This combination of soothing and moisturizing ingredients helps to tame frizz and leave hair looking healthy and vibrant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Elder flowers contain powerful antioxidants that help protect the hair shaft from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and flyaways by keeping the cuticle smooth.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elderflowers and berries contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and dryness that can contribute to frizzy hair.

Vitamins and minerals: Elder creams are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and magnesium, which help nourish and moisturize the hair, leaving it soft, smooth, and less prone to frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps associated with frizzy hair.
Barks: Elder barks contain flavonoids that can help strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz by promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, carefully drying them in a cool dark place for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with two parts coconut oil, and six parts shea butter, stirring well.
Transfer the mixture to a heat-proof container and warm it in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl until the ingredients melt completely, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the solids, and discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours before using it on your hair, and store any excess in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with frizzy hair because its soothing properties calm the scalp, reducing inflammation that can lead to frizz. The cream's moisturizing ingredients also help lock in hydration, smoothing the cuticle layer of the hair shaft and minimizing flyaways. Additionally, lavender's natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help regulate the oil production in the scalp, preventing excessive sebum buildup that can contribute to frizzy hair. This results in a more manageable, silky-smooth mane that looks healthy and radiant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizz and dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and irritation in the scalp, helping to soothe and calm frizzy hair.
Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial agents help combat bacteria and fungi that can cause dandruff, itchiness, and other scalp irritations that contribute to frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm irritated scalps while reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids and essential vitamins, seeds nourish and moisturize the hair follicles, leaving hair smooth, manageable, and frizz-free.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 2 ounces of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers, leaving them whole or torn into small pieces.
Combine the dried flowers with 4 ounces of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar, ensuring they are completely submerged.
Steep the mixture for 8 hours at room temperature, allowing the plant essence to infuse into the oil slowly and thoroughly.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the infused oil.
Whip 6 ounces of heavy cream with 2 teaspoons of the infused oil until it becomes light and fluffy, adding texture and nourishment to hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique combination of nutrients and properties. The plant's high silica content helps to strengthen hair follicles, reducing damage caused by heat styling tools and environmental stressors. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing and hydrating properties help to smooth the cuticle layer, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. As a result, field horsetail creams can leave your hair looking silky, manageable, and healthy, even in humid environments.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail cream's high silica content helps to strengthen hair follicles, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in horsetail cream help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizz and dryness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the scalp and reduce irritation, which can contribute to frizzy hair by disrupting the natural moisture balance of the hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail plant are rich in silicon and silica, which helps to strengthen and smooth hair cuticles, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Rhyzomes: Field horsetail rhyzomes contain a high concentration of antioxidants that help to repair and protect damaged hair, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried equisetum arvense leaves and stems.
Grind the herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and stir well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container and allow it to cool completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme creams helps with frizzy hair because its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties work to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation that can lead to frizz. The cream's moisturizing agents also help to lock in hydration, leaving hair feeling soft and silky. Additionally, creeping thyme's ability to regulate sebum production helps to balance the scalp's natural oil output, leading to a sleeker, more manageable texture that minimizes frizz and flyaways.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thymus vulgaris, also known as creeping thyme, contains antioxidants that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in creeping thyme creams can help soothe and calm irritated scalps and hair follicles, reducing inflammation that can lead to frizzy hair.

Antimicrobial properties: Creeping thyme's antimicrobial properties can help control the growth of fungi, bacteria, and other microorganisms on the scalp and hair, which can cause irritation, itchiness, and frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizz-causing inflammation.
Flowers: With their high content of flavonoids and terpenes, flowers provide natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that control fungal growth on the scalp, preventing frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality thymus serpyllum leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal essential oil content.
Dry the fresh thymus serpyllum leaves in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days or until crispy and brittle.
Grind 20 grams of dried thymus serpyllum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 100 milliliters of coconut oil with the ground thymus serpyllum powder in a clean glass jar, stirring until fully incorporated and smooth.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 weeks at room temperature, shaking daily, before straining through cheesecloth into a clean container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for frizzy hair are a type of hair care product that helps to smooth and calm unruly locks, reducing frizz and flyaways. They work by nourishing and protecting the hair shaft, while also providing a natural barrier against humidity and heat. The best herbal creams for frizzy hair include Rosmarinus officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe barbadensis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cassia auriculata, Santalum album, Aloe vera, Mentha x piperita, Urtica dioica, and Ocimum basilicum. If left untreated, frizzy hair can lead to self-consciousness, low self-esteem, and even social isolation, making it difficult to enjoy daily activities and maintain a healthy work-life balance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frizzy hair that are treated with herbal creams are dry, irritated, and inflamed skin conditions. Conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis can be soothed by topical herbal creams containing ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile. These herbs help reduce redness, itchiness, and inflammation, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of flaky scales or dry patches. Herbal creams are also effective in treating acne, rosacea, and minor burns, offering an natural alternative to harsh chemical treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with frizzy hair is a blend of coconut oil, argan oil, and shea butter. Add a few drops of peppermint essential oil to reduce frizz and promote smoothness. Rosemary essential oil can also be added to improve hair growth and reduce dandruff. Finally, mix in some aloe vera gel to soothe and moisturize the hair. This combination will not only tame frizzy hair but also nourish and protect it from damage. Regular use will leave hair looking healthy and shiny.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with frizzy hair because they contain antioxidants and essential oils that help to smooth and tame unruly locks. The active compounds in rosemary oil, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, help to reduce frizz by filling in gaps between hair cuticles and leaving a glossy finish. Additionally, rosemary oil's antiseptic properties help to keep the scalp healthy, promoting a balanced environment that contributes to smoother, more manageable hair. This results in a more polished, professional-looking style.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This antioxidant helps reduce frizz by protecting hair from damage caused by free radicals, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and frizz associated with scalp conditions.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-frizz properties that help smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair, leaving it looking healthy and smooth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oil extracted from the leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis is commonly used to make creams for frizzy hair because it helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle.
Flowers: The essential oil extracted from the flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis is used to make creams for frizzy hair because it helps to reduce frizz and add shine to the hair.

Seeds: Although not as commonly used, some creams for frizzy hair may incorporate rosmarinic acid extracted from the seeds of Rosmarinus officinalis, which helps to protect the hair from damage and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 rosmarinus officinalis leaves from the garden in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours or using a food dehydrator.
Grind 5 tablespoons of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 10 tablespoons of sweet almond oil with 5 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 1 teaspoon of the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder to the melted sweet almond oil mixture slowly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with frizzy hair because they contain lemongrass oil, which has natural antifungal and astringent properties. These properties help to control oil production and reduce moisture levels in the hair, thereby reducing frizz. Additionally, the lemongrass oil in these creams helps to smooth the hair cuticle, leaving it with a sleeker and more manageable texture. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus creams can lead to smoother, shinier hair and reduced frizz, making it an effective natural solution for frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps to reduce frizz by providing an antioxidant effect that protects the hair from damage caused by free radicals.
Geraniol: Contributes to smooth and manageable hair by its moisturizing and hydrating properties, which help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz.
Beta-caryophyllene: Helps to improve hair elasticity and reduce frizz by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the hair and scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly limonene, which helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are used in hair care products due to their antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp.

Roots: The roots of Cymbopogon citratus are also used in hair care products due to their ability to control frizz and add shine to hair, possibly due to their high content of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers from a pesticide-free area after sunrise.
Steep 200g of the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers in 1l of hot water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid and discard the solids then mix it with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil.
Blend the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency then add 20 drops of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into 100g containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that provide long-lasting nourishment and hydration. The gel-like extract from the Aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to strengthen and repair damaged hair cuticles. This leads to smoother, more manageable hair that is less prone to frizz. Additionally, Aloe vera's ability to retain moisture helps to lock in moisture, leaving hair feeling soft, silky, and looking healthier and more vibrant. Regular use can help to reduce frizz and add shine to your hair.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: Help to smooth and flatten hair cuticles, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
Polysaccharides (Mucopolysaccharides): Provide moisture and hydration to the hair, helping to smooth out frizzy texture and add manageability.
Amino Acids (specifically Alanine, Glutamic acid): Help to repair and strengthen hair bonds, reducing breakage and frizz, and leaving the hair with a smooth and silky texture.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which is rich in nutrients and moisturizing properties that help to tame frizzy hair.
Seeds: They contain a high concentration of aloe emodin, a compound that helps to strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz.
Leaves: They also contain a high concentration of aloin, a compound that helps to lock in moisture and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and 10 grams of shea butter in a clean mixing bowl.
Melt 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted beeswax mixture with the aloe barbadensis gel and shea butter in the mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture for 5 minutes using a hand mixer or whisk until smooth and creamy texture forms.
Transfer the herbal aloe barbadensis cream into a clean glass jar and store in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of antioxidants and natural moisturizers. The tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation that can lead to frizzy hair. Additionally, the oil's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture, leaving hair feeling smooth, silky, and manageable. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia creams can lead to healthier-looking hair that's less prone to frizz and more resilient to styling damage.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.
Linalool: This terpene has emollient and moisturizing properties that help to hydrate and soften the hair, reducing frizz and making it more manageable.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that help to smooth and tame frizzy hair.
Essential oil from Leaves: Melaleuca alternifolia leaves contain tea tree oil, which is known for its ability to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Essential oil from Leaves (continued): Tea tree oil also helps to reduce frizz by controlling the moisture levels in the hair and scalp.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 200g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 1l of carrier oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth to remove the plant material and reserve the liquid.
Combine 100g of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the hot mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique combination of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream, derived from the herb Cassia auriculata, contains flavonoids and saponins that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and flyaways. Its moisturizing and protective properties also help to lock in moisture, leaving hair smooth and manageable. As a result, Cassia auriculata creams can help to tame frizzy hair, leaving it looking healthy and radiant.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture, reducing frizz and making hair more manageable.
Tannins: Tannins in Cassia auriculata help to smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and giving hair a sleeker appearance.
Glycosides: These compounds help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of saponins, which help to moisturize and condition the hair.
Seeds: Seeds are also used because they contain cassia oil, which has been shown to improve hair texture and reduce frizz.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used due to their high antioxidant content, which can help to protect the hair from damage and promote healthy growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of cassia auriculata leaves and flowers from a clean and pollution-free area.
Dry the harvested plant material in the shade for 7 to 10 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried cassia auriculata into a fine powder using a grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the cassia auriculata powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 15 to 20 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the mixture turns into a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of natural ingredients. The essential oils present in Santalum album, such as sandalwood oil, have been known to soothe and calm the hair follicles, reducing frizz and flyaways. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to lock in moisture, leaving the hair smooth and manageable. Regular use of Santalum album creams can lead to a reduction in frizz, resulting in healthier and more beautiful hair.
remedy_constituents: α-Santanol: Helps to reduce frizz by smoothing the hair cuticle and adding a layer of protection against moisture and humidity.
Bergamotene: Contributes to frizz reduction by stabilizing the hair's natural moisture balance and providing a calming effect on the scalp.
Santalol: Assists in taming frizzy hair by reducing the surface tension of water on the hair, making it easier to style and manage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for their soothing and moisturizing properties, which help to calm and tame frizzy hair.
Barks: Used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to control oil production and reduce frizz.
Leaves: Used for their hydrating and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to moisturize and calm the hair and scalp.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50 grams of dried santalum album in 250ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks to create infused oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate the oil.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax to 1 tablespoon of the infused oil to create a solid emulsion.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes to melt the beeswax completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before scooping it into small containers for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with frizzy hair because of its unique combination of nourishing properties. The gel-like substance contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which work together to moisturize and hydrate the hair. This helps to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and add shine to dry and damaged hair. Additionally, aloe vera's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthier hair growth. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloin helps to reduce frizz by providing a protective barrier on the hair surface, locking in moisture and reducing the cuticle's ability to absorb excess moisture.
Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the damage caused by heat styling and environmental factors, leading to smoother and less frizzy hair.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly glucomannans and arabinogalactans, help to retain moisture within the hair shaft, reducing the frizz caused by dryness and improving overall hair elasticity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of gel, which is rich in emollients and humectants that help to smooth and moisturize frizzy hair.

Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera is primarily used because it has a high water content, which helps to hydrate and nourish dry and damaged hair, reducing frizz and adding shine.

Aloe Vera Juice from Leaves: Aloe vera juice extracted from the leaves is commonly used because it contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to repair and protect hair from damage, reducing frizz and making it more manageable.
remedy_recipe: Extract aloe vera gel by cutting the aloe vera leaf and scooping out the clear gel inside.
Infuse the aloe vera gel with herbal extracts by mixing in 2 tablespoons of dried herbs.
Mix the aloe vera gel and herbal extracts with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to melt the beeswax and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with frizzy hair because of its ability to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing moisture loss and promoting a healthy hair environment. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to close the cuticle layer of the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, mentha's cooling properties can soothe an itchy scalp, allowing for a more even distribution of hair oils and reducing the appearance of frizzy hair. This leads to smoother, more manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Helps to reduce frizz by creating a smooth, protective barrier on the hair surface, preventing moisture loss and minimizing the appearance of flyaways.
Menthol: Contributes to reduced frizz by temporarily stiffening the hair cuticle, making it more manageable and less prone to tangling and unruliness.

Cineole: Helps to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to frizzy hair, while also providing a calming effect on the hair follicles to promote healthier, smoother hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves are commonly used due to their cooling and calming properties, which can help to soothe and tame frizzy hair.
Leaves: The menthol content in peppermint leaves helps to reduce frizz and add shine to the hair by closing the hair cuticles.
Leaves: The antioxidants present in peppermint leaves can help to protect the hair from damage and promote healthy hair growth, reducing the appearance of frizz.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20g of dried mentha x piperita leaves 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Stir in 5g of vitamin e oil and 10g of distilled water into the cooled mixture.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with frizzy hair because they contain urtica dioica extract, also known as stinging nettle. This ingredient has natural moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties that help to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine. The cream's ability to lock in moisture also helps to prevent dryness and breakage, leading to healthier-looking hair. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from environmental damage, resulting in a smoother, more manageable mane.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help to smooth and tame frizzy hair by reducing the cuticle's swelling and increasing the hair's elasticity.
Phenolics: They possess antioxidant properties that protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing frizz and promoting smoothness.
Sterols: These compounds help to lock in moisture and reduce the hair's porosity, resulting in a smoother, less frizzy appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants, such as quercetin and catechins, which help to reduce inflammation and repair damaged hair.
Stems: They contain urtic acid and other compounds that help to balance the pH of the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Roots: They are a source of beta-sitosterol, a plant sterol that helps to strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in early morning after dew has fallen but before heat of day rises.
Dry urtica dioica leaves and stems in a single layer in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried urtica dioica with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Steep mixture for 4-6 hours then strain through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container.
Whip 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil and add to herbal cream base stirring until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum creams helps with frizzy hair because the antioxidants and essential oils present in the herb help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to frizz. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to lock in moisture and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing the appearance of frizz and flyaways. Additionally, Ocimum basilicum's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help to strengthen the hair and improve its overall texture and manageability. Regular use can lead to smoother, more manageable hair.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: This phenolic compound helps to reduce frizz by acting as an antioxidant, protecting the hair from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors.
Linalool: This terpene helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine, while also providing a soothing effect on the scalp.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid helps to stabilize the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways by creating a protective barrier against moisture and humidity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Basil leaves are commonly used in hair creams due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to control frizz and promote healthy hair.
Stems: The stems of Ocimum basilicum are rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the hair from damage and reduce frizz.
Seeds: Basil seeds are known for their ability to retain moisture and provide hydration to the hair, helping to reduce frizz and add shine.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried ocimum basilicum leaves, 100g of base cream, 50g of distilled water and 5g of glycerin.
Infuse 10g of dried ocimum basilicum leaves in 50g of distilled water for 10 minutes at 90 celsius.
Mix 50g of base cream with 10g of infused ocimum basilicum liquid and 5g of glycerin.
Heat the mixture to 70 celsius for 5 minutes and then let it cool to 40 celsius.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and creamy.
